title: Classic Latke Recipe
author: Lenny Teytelman
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.baxfifjn

steps.title: Do not peel
steps.instruction: Do not peel! No need to peel! Wash 6 Russet potatoes.

steps.title: With either food processor or hand...
steps.instruction: With either food processor or hand grater, use the coarse disc/side to grate the potatoes and the onion into the bowl hand (cheese) grater

steps.title: Transfer potato/onion mixture into a cloth...
steps.instruction: Transfer potato/onion mixture into a cloth towel and squeeze all the moisture possible. (this is a critical step for the latkes to come out crispy).

steps.title: Transfer the mixture back into the bawl
steps.instruction: Transfer the mixture back into the bawl

steps.title: Add salt, pepper, baking powder and eggs, mix well
steps.instruction: Add salt, pepper, baking powder and eggs, mix well.

steps.title: Cover bottom of a frying pan...
steps.instruction: Cover bottom of a frying pan with oil and heat to medium-high (about ¼ inch thick). image above...not nearly enough oil. Go big or go home.

steps.title: One tablespoon will yield one latke
steps.instruction: One tablespoon will yield one latke. Depending on the size of the pan, you'll be able to fit 6-10 latkes. Scoop up the mixture, place it on the hot pan. Turn the latke after 4-5 minutes (or when the edges turn golden brown). Fry for additional 4-5 minutes on the other side.

steps.title: Place the latkes on the large...
steps.instruction: Place the latkes on the large plate lined with a paper towel. Sprinkle a bit of salt after every batch. Enjoy!
